methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Nuclearn.ai is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $10.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers: three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

A peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy sector—only one same-stage peer and one adjacent-stage peer were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold. Consequently, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total raised ($10.5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to follow-on progression yields an implied valuation of $21M.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $18M to $24M, with a median of $21M. This range reflects primarily the single stage-progression method and carries material uncertainty given the sparse peer set in nuclear energy. Key risks to valuation include the early-stage nature of the sector, limited public company precedent, and the wide dispersion of business models and technology maturity across the private comp set. Additional comparable data or milestone-based revenue traction would narrow this range materially.
